title: Day 43: Succession Vacuum Enters Seventh Week; O3 Council Holds; El Jardinero and O3 Both at Large
detail: Day 43 since El Mencho's killing at Tapalpa, Jalisco (February 22, 2026). The CJNG succession vacuum remains unresolved as the seventh week concludes. O3 (Juan Carlos Valencia González) maintains organizational cohesion under the collective council structure backed by Grupo Élite's paramilitary capacity. El Jardinero (Audias Flores Silva) — primary U.S. high-value targeting objective with a $5 million State Department bounty — remains at large despite sustained post-Tapalpa pressure operations. Cultural intelligence indicators remain active: narcocorridos continue naming El Jardinero as a successor figure, reflecting unresolved internal competition. Heraclio Guerrero Martínez ('Tío Lako') continues logistics operations. Operativo Enjambre cumulative total holds at 64+ officials detained. Operativo Semana Santa Segura 2026 runs until April 12. U.S.-Mexico Joint Intelligence Targeting Center (JITC-CC) maintains high-tempo intelligence sharing. The post-Semana Santa transition period will be watched closely for any resumption of inter-cartel violence or succession-related posturing by competing CJNG factions.
